story.asA: a developer implementing the Recipe conversion engine
story.iWant: a robust XMP XML generator that creates valid Adobe Lightroom CC preset files from UniversalRecipe data
story.soThat: users can convert presets from other formats (NP3, lrtemplate) to XMP with 95%+ accuracy
story.tasks:
- Task 1: Design XMP XML structure and marshaling strategy (AC: FR-1) - 1.1: Define XMP document struct types for XML marshaling - 1.2: Determine namespace declaration strategy - 1.3: Plan XML formatting approach (indentation, whitespace) - 1.4: Review Adobe XMP specification for compliance
- Task 2: Implement core Generate() function (AC: FR-1, FR-2, FR-3) - 2.1: Create function signature matching Tech Spec - 2.2: Validate UniversalRecipe is not nil - 2.3: Build XMP structure with namespace declarations - 2.4: Map core parameters (Exposure, Contrast, Highlights, Shadows, Whites, Blacks) - 2.5: Map color parameters (Saturation, Vibrance, Clarity, Sharpness, Temperature, Tint) - 2.6: Implement inline range validation for all parameters
- Task 3: Implement HSL color generation (AC: FR-4) - 3.1: Map Red, Orange, Yellow, Green color adjustments - 3.2: Map Aqua, Blue, Purple, Magenta color adjustments - 3.3: Validate HSL ranges for all colors
- Task 4: Implement advanced features generation (AC: FR-5) - 4.1: Generate Split Toning parameters (Shadow/Highlight Hue and Saturation) - 4.2: Generate Tone Curve array if present - 4.3: Handle optional/missing fields gracefully
- Task 5: Implement error handling with ConversionError (AC: FR-7, NFR-3) - 5.1: Reuse ConversionError type from parse.go - 5.2: Wrap all errors with Operation="generate", Format="xmp" - 5.3: Include field names in error context
- Task 6: Implement XML marshaling and formatting (AC: FR-1, FR-6) - 6.1: Use encoding/xml.MarshalIndent for proper formatting - 6.2: Add XML declaration header - 6.3: Validate output is well-formed XML - 6.4: Test output loads in Lightroom CC
- Task 7: Write comprehensive tests (AC: NFR-2) - 7.1: Create table-driven tests using testdata/xmp/ samples - 7.2: Implement round-trip tests (XMP → parse → generate → compare) - 7.3: Add edge case tests (nil recipe, invalid ranges, missing fields) - 7.4: Create performance benchmarks targeting <30ms - 7.5: Validate test coverage ≥90%
- Task 8: Documentation and code quality (AC: NFR-3) - 8.1: Add GoDoc comments to Generate() function - 8.2: Document XMP structure mapping in code comments - 8.3: Run gofmt and go vet - 8.4: Verify code follows all architectural patterns

story-context.acceptanceCriteria:
**Functional Requirements**
**FR-1: XMP File Generation** - Generate valid XML structure with proper indentation and formatting - Include required Adobe XMP namespace declarations (xmlns:x, xmlns:crs, xmlns:rdf) - Output XML declaration: - Generated files open in Lightroom CC without errors or warnings
**FR-2: Core Parameter Generation (Basic Adjustments)** - Generate crs:Exposure2012 from UniversalRecipe.Exposure (-5.0 to +5.0) - Generate crs:Contrast2012 from UniversalRecipe.Contrast (-100 to +100) - Generate crs:Highlights2012, Shadows2012, Whites2012, Blacks2012 (-100 to +100 each)
**FR-3: Color Parameter Generation** - Generate crs:Saturation, Vibrance, Clarity2012, Sharpness, Temperature, Tint - Ranges: Saturation/Vibrance/Clarity (-100 to +100), Sharpness (0 to 150), Temperature/Tint (-100 to +100)
**FR-4: HSL Color Adjustments Generation** - Generate HSL adjustments for all 8 colors (Red, Orange, Yellow, Green, Aqua, Blue, Purple, Magenta) - For each color: crs:Hue{Color}, crs:Saturation{Color}, crs:Luminance{Color} (-100 to +100)
**FR-5: Advanced Features Generation** - Generate crs:ToneCurve from UniversalRecipe.ToneCurve (array of Point{X, Y}) - Generate Split Toning parameters (Shadow/Highlight Hue 0-360, Saturation 0-100)
**FR-6: Data Type Handling** - Format floating-point values correctly (Exposure: float64, 2 decimals) - Format integer values correctly - Handle zero values gracefully (omit or include based on XMP spec) - Validate all numeric values are within expected ranges before generation
**FR-7: Error Handling** - Return clear error if UniversalRecipe is nil - Return clear error for parameters outside valid ranges - Wrap all errors in ConversionError with context (Operation="generate", Format="xmp") - Include field name in error messages for debugging
**Non-Functional Requirements**
**NFR-1: Performance** - Generate single XMP file in <30ms - Validation via Go benchmarks
**NFR-2: Test Coverage** - Generate XMP from all 913 UniversalRecipe samples - Test coverage ≥90% for xmp package - Table-driven tests following Pattern 7 - Round-trip validation: XMP → parse → generate → compare (±1 tolerance)
**NFR-3: Code Quality** - Follow Pattern 4: File structure (generate.go in xmp package) - Follow Pattern 5: Error handling with ConversionError wrapper - Follow Pattern 6: Inline validation, fail fast - Use stdlib encoding/xml package only (zero external dependencies) - Code passes gofmt and go vet without warnings

doc.title: Technical Specification - Epic 1: Core Conversion Engine
doc.snippet: XMP Format: XML/RDF structure with Adobe XMP namespaces. Parameters: 50+ including Exposure, Contrast, HSL (8 colors × 3 properties), Tone Curves, Split Toning. Generate function signature: func Generate(recipe *model.UniversalRecipe) ([]byte, error). Uses encoding/xml.MarshalIndent for XML generation with namespace declarations.

doc.title: Technical Specification - Epic 1: Core Conversion Engine
doc.snippet: Acceptance criteria: Generate XMP files that load in Lightroom CC without errors. Preserve namespace declarations (xmlns:crs, xmlns:rdf, xmlns:x). Round-trip conversion produces semantically identical output (±1 tolerance). Test with 913 sample XMP files.

doc.title: Technical Specification - Epic 1: Core Conversion Engine
doc.snippet: Table-driven tests with 913 XMP sample files from testdata/xmp/. Coverage target: ≥90% for all format packages. Round-trip testing validates ≥95% accuracy for bidirectional conversions using Pattern 7 table-driven test structure.

doc.title: Technical Specification - Epic 1: Core Conversion Engine
doc.snippet: XMP generate: <20ms target using encoding/xml stdlib. Optimization: avoid unnecessary allocations, no goroutines for sub-100ms operations. Use xml.MarshalIndent for formatted output.

doc.title: Architecture - Design Patterns and Standards
doc.snippet: All conversion errors wrapped in ConversionError type with Operation, Format, Field, and Cause fields. Generator must use Operation="generate", Format="xmp". Type-safe error checking with Unwrap() support for errors.Is/As.

doc.title: Architecture - Design Patterns and Standards
doc.snippet: Table-driven tests using filepath.Glob to iterate testdata samples. Each test validates critical fields with range checks. Run tests with: go test -cover ./... for coverage reporting.

doc.title: Product Requirements Document
doc.snippet: Code quality: gofmt, golint, go vet must pass. Test coverage: 90%+ for core conversion logic. Self-documenting names, comments for complex logic. Modular design with pluggable format adapters.

artifact.reason: Generator MUST reuse this exact error type for consistency. Wrap all errors with Operation="generate", Format="xmp".
Generator should mirror this struct for XML marshaling. Contains all 50+ XMP attributes with xml tags. Reference for namespace declarations and structure.
This is the INPUT to Generate() function. Map these fields to XMP attributes. Note: Temperature is *int (nullable pointer).
HSL color adjustments structure. Generator extracts Hue, Saturation, Luminance for each of 8 colors and maps to crs:Hue{Color}, crs:Saturation{Color}, crs:Luminance{Color}.
Generator should validate UniversalRecipe input before generating XMP. Use these functions for range checking: Exposure (-5.0 to +5.0), percentages (-100 to +100), HSL ranges.
Reference template for generator output. Shows decimal precision (floats: "1.50"), integer formatting ("25"), namespace declarations, and complete XMP structure.
Generator tests should mirror these patterns. Round-trip tests will use Parse() to validate generated XMP. Reference for table-driven test structure and error handling tests.

constraints.constraint: Use stdlib encoding/xml package only - zero external dependencies required
Reuse ConversionError type from parse.go (lines 43-61) for all error handling
Follow Pattern 4: generate.go must be in internal/formats/xmp/ package
Follow Pattern 5: Wrap all errors with Operation="generate", Format="xmp", include Field name
Follow Pattern 6: Inline validation, fail fast on invalid UniversalRecipe input
Follow Pattern 7: Table-driven tests using testdata/xmp/ samples
Handle Temperature as *int (nullable) - omit if nil or use default value
Output minimal XMP format matching testdata/xmp/sample.xmp, not full Adobe metadata
Decimal precision: float64 values formatted with 2 decimal places (%.2f)
Code must pass gofmt and go vet without warnings

interface.name: UniversalRecipe to XMP Mapping
interface.signature: Exposure → crs:Exposure2012, Contrast → crs:Contrast2012, Red.Hue → crs:HueRed, Temperature(*int) → crs:Temperature (handle nil)

tests.standards: Testing follows Pattern 7 (table-driven tests with real samples). Framework: Go's built-in testing package. Coverage target: ≥90% for xmp package. Benchmark target: <30ms for single Generate() call. Test structure: TestGenerate(), TestGenerateRoundTrip(), TestGenerateInvalidInput(), BenchmarkGenerate(). Use filepath.Glob("../../../testdata/xmp/*.xmp") to iterate all samples. Validation: Parse generated XMP and compare to original UniversalRecipe (±1 tolerance for rounding). Error tests: validate ConversionError wrapping with correct Operation/Format/Field values.

ideas.idea: Test 1: Generate valid XMP with all namespace declarations - verify xml.Unmarshal succeeds and namespaces present
Test 2: Validate XML declaration header is present: <?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
Test 3: Verify proper indentation and formatting using xml.MarshalIndent with two-space indent

Test 4: Generate core parameters - validate Exposure2012, Contrast2012, Highlights2012, Shadows2012, Whites2012, Blacks2012, Saturation, Vibrance, Clarity, Sharpness, Temperature, Tint all present in output
Test 5: Generate HSL adjustments for all 8 colors - verify crs:HueRed, crs:SaturationRed, crs:LuminanceRed (and similar for Orange, Yellow, Green, Aqua, Blue, Purple, Magenta)
Test 6: Generate Split Toning parameters - validate Shadow/Highlight Hue (0-360) and Saturation (0-100)
Test 7: Validate float formatting - Exposure should be "1.50" (2 decimals), not "1.5"
Test 8: Validate integer formatting - Contrast should be "25", not "25.00"
Test 9: Test zero value handling - verify zeros are included or omitted correctly per XMP spec
Test 10: Validate range checking before generation - out-of-range Exposure (-6.0) should error
Test 11: Test nil UniversalRecipe input - should return ConversionError with Operation="generate", Format="xmp"
Test 12: Test out-of-range parameters - Contrast=150 should return error with Field="Contrast"
Test 13: Validate ConversionError wrapping - all errors include Operation, Format, Field (when applicable), and Cause
Test 14: Benchmark Generate() performance - should complete in <30ms for single file
Test 15: Round-trip test - Parse XMP → Generate XMP → Parse again → compare recipes (±1 tolerance)
Test 16: Test all 913 samples from testdata/xmp/ - generate XMP from parsed recipe and validate output
Test 17: Table-driven tests for edge cases: nil Temperature (nullable), missing optional fields, all zeros
Test 18: Verify generate.go in correct location (internal/formats/xmp/)
Test 19: Run gofmt on generate.go - should produce no changes
Test 20: Run go vet on xmp package - should produce no warnings
